salutation: To the team I hope to join,

paragraphs.opening: I want to give you a sense of what it's like to work with me - not just my skills on paper, but the day-to-day reality of having me on your team.
paragraphs.reputation: In my past workplaces, I carried a reputation: the reliable one. That's not an empty phrase. People actively sought me out when they needed something done right. That forced me to protect it. Tasks were finished on time, or communicated early when tradeoffs appeared. Code was written cleanly, thoughtfully, and rarely buggy. And when a bug did appear, the time from report to deployed fix was short. The reputation didn't weigh me down; it pushed me to be better every single day. You could hold me to that same standard, and I would respect it.
paragraphs.unemployed: I've been unemployed for about six months now. That's a long time, and I don't take it lightly. But I haven't stopped building - because building is part of who I am.
paragraphs.reading: I've also been reading, trying to become a better engineer: Modern Software Engineering, Accelerate (the DevOps book). And here's something—it's actually painful to read these books when you're out of work. They're all about things you do when you're working every day. So I'm sitting here with a head full of ideas I can't fully apply yet. Take trunk-based development, for example: committing to main branch every day, even if the full feature isn't done. That's a great idea I learned from the books, and one I am eager to try on a real team again.
paragraphs.energy: I am very excited to start working again. I bring energy. I bring ideas. And I bring the same reliability that defined me before - except sharper now, because six months of reading and building have given me new lenses.
paragraphs.assessment: If you want to give me an assessment, I'm confident in my ability to produce good work. I'd rather show you than just tell you.
paragraphs.closing: May we have a chance to collaborate.

projects.name: Bigcampus
projects.description: an app that removes friction from socializing

projects.name: Collective Chess
projects.description: a Reddit-style discussion platform for chess positions
